name: Hike from Gerena to Salteras – circular route on the Water Route
name_original: Gerena desde Salteras – circular por la Ruta del Agua
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Valencina De La Concepción are situated in the Aljarafe region, characterized by gentle hills and fertile plains, offering varied terrain for cyclists. The area is recognized for its ecological value, with routes through beautiful landscapes. Its proximity to Seville provides access to broader cycling networks, including "Vías Verdes" and the historic Via de la Plata. The landscape includes holm and cork oak meadows, and riparian vegetation along rivers, particularly towards the Sierra Morena. Best touring cycling routes around Valencina De La Concepción The most popular touring cycling route is Hike from Gerena to Salteras – circular route on the Water Route, a 24.3 miles (39.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 51 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the scenic Water Route. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is La Coriana Spring – Dolmen de La Pastora loop from Camas, a difficult 38.9 miles (62.7 km) path. This route explores a spring and the ancient Dolmen de La Pastora. Local touring cyclists also love the Alamillo Bridge – Barqueta Bridge loop from Salteras, a 26.4 miles (42.5 km) trail leading through urban and natural landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 37 minutes. Touring cycling around Valencina De La Concepción is defined by gentle hills, fertile plains, and access to historic routes like the Via de la Plata. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more difficult long-distance paths. The routes in Valencina De La Concepción are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Valencina De La Concepción's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Valencina De La Concepción?
A: There are over 30 touring cycling routes around Valencina De La Concepción, offering a variety of options for different skill levels and preferences. The komoot community has rated these routes highly, with an average score of 4.4 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes near Valencina De La Concepción?
A: The area around Valencina De La Concepción, situated in the Aljarafe region, is characterized by gentle hills and fertile plains. You'll encounter routes through beautiful landscapes with high ecological value, including holm and cork oak meadows, and riparian vegetation along rivers, especially as you head towards the Sierra Morena.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Valencina De La Concepción?
A: Andalusia, where Valencina De La Concepción is located, offers ideal weather conditions for cycle tourism year-round. However, spring (March-May) and autumn (September-November) generally provide the most pleasant temperatures for longer rides.

Q: Are there options for less experienced touring cyclists?
A: Yes, there are several routes suitable for beginners. Out of the over 30 routes available, 3 are classified as easy, providing a gentle introduction to touring cycling in the region. Many routes offer varied terrain, from moderate loops to more challenging long-distance paths.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or archaeological sites I can visit while cycling?
A: Absolutely. Valencina De La Concepción is known for its significant Chalcolithic deposits. You can visit impressive sites like the Pastora and Matarrubilla Dolmens. For a broader historical experience, nearby attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1055590" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Real Alcázar of Seville&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/8110953" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Saint George&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The region boasts beautiful natural landscapes. You can explore areas with high ecological value, including holm and cork oak meadows. Nearby natural monuments include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7365230" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monumental Carob Tree in María Luisa Park&lt;/a&gt; and the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7365263" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Murillo Gardens&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990365996/hike-from-gerena-to-salteras-circular-route-on-the-water-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hike from Gerena to Salteras – circular route on the Water Route&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 24.3-mile (39.1 km) option. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10300398" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alamillo Bridge – Barqueta Bridge loop from Salteras&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 26.4 miles (42.5 km).

Q: Can I access touring cycling routes using public transport?
A: Valencina De La Concepción's proximity to Seville means you can often connect to broader cycling networks and routes via public transport links from the city. While specific public transport access points for individual routes might vary, Seville serves as a major hub for reaching the wider Aljarafe region and beyond.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling options from Valencina De La Concepción?
A: Yes, Valencina's location provides access to significant long-distance routes. You can connect to the historic Via de la Plata, which offers hundreds of kilometers of cycling through impressive landscapes. The region also provides access to the extensive network of "Vías Verdes" (Greenways) and the "Transandalus" route.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes in the area?
A: For those seeking a challenge, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1276627" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Coriana Spring – Dolmen de La Pastora loop from Camas&lt;/a&gt; offer a difficult 38.9-mile (62.7 km) path exploring a spring and ancient dolmens. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38178911" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Water Route loop from Camas&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 72.3-mile (116.4 km) ride.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that pass through scenic viewpoints?
A: While specific named viewpoints are not detailed for every route, the Aljarafe region's gentle hills and varied landscapes naturally offer many scenic vistas. Routes often traverse areas with high ecological value, providing picturesque views of holm and cork oak meadows and riparian vegetation.

Q: Can I find family-friendly touring cycling routes in Valencina De La Concepción?
A: Yes, the presence of bike lanes within Valencina and the generally gentle terrain of the Aljarafe region make it suitable for family cycling. Look for the routes classified as 'easy' in the komoot guide, which are typically shorter and have less elevation gain, making them ideal for families.
tips: Views of the old granite mine and the Sevillian countryside. The granite extraction activity was the main economic activity of the municipality years ago, since the town of Gerena is located on a granite massif.
The Water Route is a 68-kilometer route that surrounds the city of Seville. It begins in the Carambolo and ends in the Lagos del Serrano, near the Embalse de Cala.
